target.name: Form Energy
target.founder: Mateo Jaramillo, Yet-Ming Chiang, Ted Wiley, William Woodford, Marco Ferrara

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
Form Energy, a Series G climate and energy technology company, is assessed against a peer universe of 9 comparables (1 public, 8 private) spanning similar-stage and same-sector operators including TAE Technologies, Helion, Sila Nanotechnologies, and Watershed.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the company's last post-money valuation of $3.5B and applying a typical Series G-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.25x, yields $4.4B. Peer-median methodology across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (TAE at $6.0B, Helion at $5.4B, Sila at $3.3B, Watershed at $1.8B) produces a median of $4.3B, with a peer range of $1.8B–$6.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3.7B (low) to $5.0B (high), with a median of $4.4B. This represents approximately 0–43% upside from the current $3.5B post-money. Key uncertainties include product commercialization timelines, long-duration energy storage market adoption rates, and capital intensity of scaling manufacturing—all material variables not fully captured in comparable company multiples. The peer set's wide dispersion ($1.8B–$6.0B) reflects heterogeneous technology risk and development stage within the climate-tech sector.
